Funding for Pilot Projects To Award Grants for Tribal School Lunch Programs, , , provided that:Pub. L. 118–42, div. B, title VII, § 758138 Stat. 114Mar. 9, 2024“(a)For an additional amount for the Office of the Secretary, $2,000,000, to remain available until expended, for the Secretary of Agriculture to carry out no more than 10 pilot projects, under the terms and conditions determined by the Secretary for a period not to exceed 2 years, that award grants to an Indian tribe; a tribal organization approved by an Indian tribe; a tribal educational agency; a consortium of Indian tribes; or a partnership between an Indian tribe and either a State educational agency, a local educational agency, a tribal educational agency, or the Bureau of Indian Education to operate and implement the school lunch program as authorized by [section 18 of] the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act (), the summer food service program as established under section 13 of the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act [], the child and adult care food program as established by section 17 of the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act [], or the school breakfast program established by [section 4 of] the Child Nutrition Act of 1966 () in either a Bureau-funded school (as defined in section 1141 of the Education Amendments of 1978 ()); a school (as defined in section 12(d) of the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act () on or near an Indian reservation; or an early child care and education facility: , That to carry out this pilot program each grant awarded shall be no less than $10,000 and no more than $100,000 for each school year and shall not increase state administrative costs or the amount of benefits provided in any program: , That the term ‘Indian tribe’ has the meaning given the term in section 4 of the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act ().42 U.S.C. 176942 U.S.C. 176142 U.S.C. 176642 U.S.C. 177325 U.S.C. 202142 U.S.C. 1760(d)25 U.S.C. 5304ProvidedProvided further“(b)Notwithstanding any other provision of law, a pilot project grant recipient shall be reimbursed for meals served under the school lunch program, the summer food service program, and the child and adult care food program as if the recipient were a State under the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act [ et seq.]; and under the school breakfast program as if the recipient were a State educational agency.42 U.S.C. 1751“(c)Not later than 1 year after the conclusion of the pilot program, the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report on the outcomes of the pilot program.”

Other Demonstration Projects for Feeding Homeless Children, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 102–342, title I, § 101(b)106 Stat. 911Pub. L. 105–336, title I, § 109(c)(2)112 Stat. 3157Aug. 14, 1992Oct. 31, 1998“The Secretary of Agriculture may conduct demonstration projects to identify effective means of providing food assistance to homeless children residing in temporary shelters.”

Alternative Counting and Claiming Procedures; Promulgation of Regulations, , , provided that not later than , Secretary of Agriculture was to issue final regulations to implement subsec. (g) of this section.Pub. L. 101–147, title II, § 205(b)103 Stat. 911Nov. 10, 1989July 1, 1990

Funding for Pilot Projects To Award Grants for Tribal School Lunch Programs, , , provided that:Pub. L. 118–42, div. B, title VII, § 758138 Stat. 114Mar. 9, 2024“(a)For an additional amount for the Office of the Secretary, $2,000,000, to remain available until expended, for the Secretary of Agriculture to carry out no more than 10 pilot projects, under the terms and conditions determined by the Secretary for a period not to exceed 2 years, that award grants to an Indian tribe; a tribal organization approved by an Indian tribe; a tribal educational agency; a consortium of Indian tribes; or a partnership between an Indian tribe and either a State educational agency, a local educational agency, a tribal educational agency, or the Bureau of Indian Education to operate and implement the school lunch program as authorized by [section 18 of] the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act (), the summer food service program as established under section 13 of the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act [], the child and adult care food program as established by section 17 of the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act [], or the school breakfast program established by [section 4 of] the Child Nutrition Act of 1966 () in either a Bureau-funded school (as defined in section 1141 of the Education Amendments of 1978 ()); a school (as defined in section 12(d) of the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act () on or near an Indian reservation; or an early child care and education facility: , That to carry out this pilot program each grant awarded shall be no less than $10,000 and no more than $100,000 for each school year and shall not increase state administrative costs or the amount of benefits provided in any program: , That the term ‘Indian tribe’ has the meaning given the term in section 4 of the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act ().42 U.S.C. 176942 U.S.C. 176142 U.S.C. 176642 U.S.C. 177325 U.S.C. 202142 U.S.C. 1760(d)25 U.S.C. 5304ProvidedProvided further“(b)Notwithstanding any other provision of law, a pilot project grant recipient shall be reimbursed for meals served under the school lunch program, the summer food service program, and the child and adult care food program as if the recipient were a State under the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act [ et seq.]; and under the school breakfast program as if the recipient were a State educational agency.42 U.S.C. 1751“(c)Not later than 1 year after the conclusion of the pilot program, the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report on the outcomes of the pilot program.”

Alternative Counting and Claiming Procedures; Promulgation of Regulations, , , provided that not later than , Secretary of Agriculture was to issue final regulations to implement subsec. (g) of this section.Pub. L. 101–147, title II, § 205(b)103 Stat. 911Nov. 10, 1989July 1, 1990

children.heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 111–296, title IV, § 441(a)(9)124 Stat. 3264Dec. 13, 2010
children.label: a Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 111–296, title IV, § 441(a)(9)124 Stat. 3264Dec. 13, 2010

children.heading: Extension of eligibility of certain school districts to receive cash or commodity letters of credit assistance for school lunch programs
children.label: b Extension of eligibility of certain school districts to receive cash or commodity letters of credit assistance for school lunch programs

children.text: Upon request to the Secretary, any school district that on , was receiving all cash payments or all commodity letters of credit in lieu of entitlement commodities for its school lunch program shall receive all cash payments or all commodity letters of credit in lieu of entitlement commodities for its school lunch program beginning . The Secretary, directly or through contract, shall administer the project under this subsection.January 1, 1987July 1, 1987

Any school district that elects under paragraph (1) to receive all cash payments or all commodity letters of credit in lieu of entitlement commodities for its school lunch program shall receive bonus commodities in the same manner as if such school district was receiving all entitlement commodities for its school lunch program.

children.heading: Alternative counting and claiming procedures
children.label: c Alternative counting and claiming procedures

children.text: The Secretary may conduct pilot projects to test alternative counting and claiming procedures.
Each pilot program carried out under this subsection shall be evaluated by the Secretary after it has been in operation for 3 years.

children.heading: Access to local foods: Patrick Leahy Farm to School Program
children.label: g Access to local foods: Patrick Leahy Farm to School Program

children.heading: Definition of eligible school
children.label: 1 Definition of eligible school
children.text: In this subsection, the term “eligible school” means a school or institution that participates in a program under this chapter or the school breakfast program established under .section 1773 of this title

children.heading: Program
children.label: 2 Program
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out a program to assist eligible schools, State and local agencies, Indian tribal organizations, agricultural producers or groups of agricultural producers, and nonprofit entities through grants and technical assistance to implement farm to school programs that improve access to local foods in eligible schools.

children.label: i
children.text: training;

children.label: ii
children.text: supporting operations;

children.label: iii
children.text: planning;

children.label: iv
children.text: purchasing equipment;

children.label: v
children.text: developing school gardens;

children.label: vi
children.text: developing partnerships; and

children.label: vii
children.text: implementing farm to school programs.

children.label: i
children.text: geographical diversity; and

children.label: ii
children.text: equitable treatment of urban, rural, and tribal communities.

children.heading: Maximum amount
children.label: C Maximum amount
children.text: The total amount provided to a grant recipient under this subsection shall not exceed $100,000.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Federal share of costs for a project funded through a grant awarded under this subsection shall not exceed 75 percent of the total cost of the project.

children.heading: Federal matching
children.label: B Federal matching
children.text: As a condition of receiving a grant under this subsection, a grant recipient shall provide matching support in the form of cash or in-kind contributions, including facilities, equipment, or services provided by State and local governments, nonprofit organizations, and private sources.

children.label: A
children.text: make local food products available on the menu of the eligible school;

children.label: B
children.text: serve a high proportion of children who are eligible for free or reduced price lunches;

children.label: C
children.text: incorporate experiential nutrition education activities in curriculum planning that encourage the participation of school children in farm and garden-based agricultural education activities;

children.label: D
children.text: demonstrate collaboration between eligible schools, nongovernmental and community-based organizations, agricultural producer groups, and other community partners;

children.label: E
children.text: include adequate and participatory evaluation plans;

children.label: F
children.text: demonstrate the potential for long-term program sustainability; and

children.label: G
children.text: meet any other criteria that the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.heading: Evaluation
children.label: 6 Evaluation
children.text: As a condition of receiving a grant under this subsection, each grant recipient shall agree to cooperate in an evaluation by the Secretary of the program carried out using grant funds.

children.label: A
children.text: to facilitate the coordination and sharing of information and resources in the Department that may be applicable to the farm to school program;

children.label: B
children.text: to collect and share information on best practices; and

children.label: C
children.text: to disseminate research and data on existing farm to school programs and the potential for programs in underserved areas.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: On , and each October 1 thereafter, out of any funds in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, the Secretary of the Treasury shall transfer to the Secretary to carry out this subsection $5,000,000, to remain available until expended.October 1, 2012

children.heading: Receipt and acceptance
children.label: B Receipt and acceptance
children.text: The Secretary shall be entitled to receive, shall accept, and shall use to carry out this subsection the funds transferred under subparagraph (A), without further appropriation.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 9 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: In addition to the amounts made available under paragraph (8), there are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this subsection such sums as are necessary for each of fiscal years 2011 through 2015.

children.heading: Pilot program for high-poverty schools
children.label: h Pilot program for high-poverty schools
I
children.text: a school-based program with hands-on vegetable gardening and nutrition education that is incorporated into the curriculum for 1 or more grades at 2 or more eligible schools; or

children.label: II
children.text: a community-based summer program with hands-on vegetable gardening and nutrition education that is part of, or coordinated with, a summer enrichment program at 2 or more eligible schools.

children.heading: Eligible school
children.label: ii Eligible school
children.text: The term “eligible school” means a public school, at least 50 percent of the students of which are eligible for free or reduced price meals under this chapter.

children.label: i
children.text: be planted, cared for, and harvested by students at the eligible schools; and

children.label: ii
children.text: teach the students participating in the community gardens about agriculture production practices and diet.

children.label: i
children.text: at least 1 State shall be among the 15 largest States, as determined by the Secretary;

children.label: ii
children.text: at least 1 State shall be among the 16th to 30th largest States, as determined by the Secretary; and

children.label: iii
children.text: at least 1 State shall be a State that is not described in clause (i) or (ii).

children.label: i
children.text: used to supplement food provided at the eligible school;

children.label: ii
children.text: distributed to students to bring home to the families of the students; or

children.label: iii
children.text: donated to a local food bank or senior center nutrition program.

children.heading: No cost-sharing requirement
children.label: E No cost-sharing requirement
children.text: A nonprofit organization or public entity that receives a grant under this paragraph shall not be required to share the cost of carrying out the activities assisted under this paragraph.

children.heading: Evaluation
children.label: F Evaluation
children.text: A nonprofit organization or public entity that receives a grant under this paragraph shall be required to cooperate in an evaluation carried out by the Secretary.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 2 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as are necessary to carry out this subsection for each of fiscal years 2004 through 2015.

children.heading: Year-round services for eligible entities
children.label: i Year-round services for eligible entities
i
children.text: during the months of May through September;

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a service institution that operates a food service program for children on school vacation, at anytime under a continuous school calendar; and

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of a service institution that provides meal service at a nonschool site to children who are not in school for a period during the school year due to a natural disaster, building repair, court order, or similar case, at anytime during such a period; and

children.label: B
children.text: for a snack served during each day of operation after school hours, weekends, and school holidays during the regular school calendar.

children.heading: Payments
children.label: 2 Payments
children.text: The service institution shall be reimbursed consistent with .section 1761(b)(1) of this title

children.heading: Administration
children.label: 3 Administration
children.text: To receive reimbursement under this subsection, a service institution shall comply with , other than subsections (b)(2) and (c)(1) of that section.section 1761 of this title

children.heading: Evaluation
children.label: 4 Evaluation
children.text: Not later than , the State agency shall submit to the Secretary a report on the effect of this subsection on participation in the summer food service program for children established under .September 30, 2007section 1761 of this title

children.heading: Funding
children.label: 5 Funding
children.text: The Secretary shall provide to the State of California such sums as are necessary to carry out this subsection for each of fiscal years 2011 through 2015.

children.heading: Free lunch and breakfast eligibility
children.label: j Free lunch and breakfast eligibility

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to the availability of funds under paragraph (4), the Secretary shall expand the service of free lunches and breakfasts provided at schools participating in the school lunch program under this chapter or the school breakfast program under in all or part of 5 States selected by the Secretary (of which at least 1 shall be a largely rural State with a significant Native American population).section 1773 of this title

children.heading: Income eligibility
children.label: 2 Income eligibility
children.text: The income guidelines for determining eligibility for free lunches or breakfasts under this subsection shall be 185 percent of the applicable family size income levels contained in the nonfarm income poverty guidelines prescribed by the Office of Management and Budget, as adjusted annually in accordance with .section 1758(b)(1)(B) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 3 years after the implementation of this subsection, the Secretary shall conduct an evaluation to assess the impact of the changed income eligibility guidelines by comparing the school food authorities operating under this subsection to school food authorities not operating under this subsection.

children.label: I
children.text: children in households with incomes less than 130 percent of the applicable family income levels contained in the nonfarm poverty income guidelines prescribed by the Office of Management and Budget, as adjusted annually in accordance with ; andsection 1758(b)(1)(B) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: children in households with incomes greater than 130 percent and not greater than 185 percent of the applicable family income levels contained in the nonfarm poverty income guidelines prescribed by the Office of Management and Budget, as adjusted annually in accordance with .section 1758(b)(1)(B) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: certification and participation rates in the school lunch and breakfast programs;

children.label: II
children.text: rates of lunch- and breakfast-skipping;

children.label: III
children.text: academic achievement;

children.label: IV
children.text: the allocation of funds authorized in title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act [ et seq.] to local educational agencies and public schools; and20 U.S.C. 6301

children.label: V
children.text: other factors determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Cost assessment
children.label: C Cost assessment
children.text: The evaluation shall assess the increased costs associated with providing additional free, reduced price, or paid meals in the school food authorities operating under this subsection.

children.heading: Report
children.label: D Report
children.text: On completion of the evaluation, the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Education and the Workforce of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate a report describing the results of the evaluation under this paragraph.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 4 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as are necessary to carry out this subsection, to remain available until expended.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish an organic food pilot program (referred to in this subsection as the “pilot program”) under which the Secretary shall provide grants on a competitive basis to school food authorities selected under paragraph (3).

children.label: i
children.text: to enter into competitively awarded contracts or cooperative agreements with school food authorities selected under paragraph (3); or

children.label: ii
children.text: to make grants to school food authority applicants selected under paragraph (3).

children.heading: School food authority uses of funds
children.label: B School food authority uses of funds
children.text: A school food authority that receives a grant under this section shall use the grant funds to establish a pilot program that increases the quantity of organic foods provided to schoolchildren under the school lunch program established under this chapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A school food authority seeking a contract, grant, or cooperative agreement under this subsection shall submit to the Secretary an application in such form, containing such information, and at such time as the Secretary shall prescribe.

children.label: i
children.text: the poverty line (as defined in , including any revision required by that section)) applicable to a family of the size involved of the households in the district served by the school food authority, giving preference to school food authority applicants in which not less than 50 percent of the households in the district are at or below the Federal poverty line;section 9902(2) of this title1So in original.1

children.label: I
children.text: to improve the nutritional value of school meals;

children.label: II
children.text: to carry out innovative programs that improve the health and wellness of schoolchildren; and

children.label: III
children.text: to evaluate the outcome of the pilot program; and

children.label: iii
children.text: any other criteria the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 4 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this subsection $10,000,000 for fiscal years 2011 through 2015.
